sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Bobby, is a 50-year-old male who presents with a 2-3 week history of right shoulder pain, which he attributes to recent physical activity in his backyard, including lifting and working on various projects.
He reports that the pain is located on the outside of the shoulder and is exacerbated by movement, but is not constant.
He denies numbness in his arm or hands.
He has a history of occasional shoulder pain due to a previous injury playing baseball.
He has been managing his symptoms with ibuprofen (Advil) and icing.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of occasional shoulder pain due to a previous injury playing baseball.
Review of systems: The patient denies any numbness in his arm or hands.
Current medications: Ibuprofen (Advil) as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are as follows: temperature 97.8°F, heart rate 62 bpm, respirations 16, blood pressure 116/84, oxygen saturation 99%.
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in the right shoulder, with tenderness at the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
There is no tenderness at the sternoclavicular or acromioclavicular joints.
The patient has a good hand grip, and on neurovascular exam of the right arm, capillary refill is less than 3 seconds and sensation to touch is intact.
Test Results: The patient's shoulder X-ray shows no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, which is an injury to the tendons of the muscles that help make the shoulder muscles.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; status: confirmed; Sore feet; status: suspected.
Plan: I prescribed Tylenol for pain relief.
I ordered an MRI to further evaluate the patient's shoulder.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for approximately 6-8 weeks.
If the patient's symptoms do not improve, we can consider a steroid injection in the shoulder.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 6-8 weeks to assess the effectiveness of the physical therapy.
